Title: Picture plugin enhancements
Author: cgwrench
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

I've been testing the picture plugin, as I'm looking to migrate from Eleventy (and it's image plugin) to Lume. There are a few additional features the Eleventy's image plugin offers that I'd love to see in Lume's picture plugin[^1]:

1. `width` and `height` attributes are added to the `img` element.
2. Images aren't scaled up beyond their original dimensions.
3. It's easy to add `deocding="async"` and `loading="lazy"` attributes to the `img` element.
4. It enforces that there is an `alt` attribute set on the `img` element.
5. A hash of the image's contents is included in the filename of generated images (to support setting long cache headers).

I thought I'd raise them here to see if any of these could be incorporated into the picture plugin. I'd be happy to try and implement some of these myself, if there is an appetite to add these.

I think item 5 is covered by https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/352, and I think it makes sense to be a separate plugin rather than something purely image related. I'd love to see some progress made on this (and would be happy to try and contribute this, but would need some guidance on the right approach to take).

I think items 3 & 4 are simple enough to do with a plugin I could write myself, so no worries if these don't become part of the picture plugin.

I don't think that item 2 is possible with the picture plugin. Is this currently possible? If not, is this a feature you'd like to see with the picture plugin?

Now for item 1, again this could be done as part of plugin I could write. However, this one gave me some pause for thought. Doing this myself would mean that I've have to read the contents of the generated images to work out their dimensions. As the imagick plugin has already read and processed the images, it seems a bit wasteful to re-read them. However, this is totally doable with another plugin that runs after the picture/imagick plugins, so it's also not a deal breaker if I have to write this myself.

Is adding `width` and `height` attributes to `img` elements something that could be done with the picture plugin?

[^1]: This has partially been covered by [this discussion](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/350). Apologies for re-raising some of these points.

Title: Ordering of `source` elements generated by the picture plugin
Author: cgwrench
Body: ### Enter your suggestions in details: I'm trying to use the picture plugin to add support for serving multiple image formats. I've found that the order of `source` elements isn't ideal. With the following in my `_config.ts`: ```typescript site.use(picture()); site.use(imagick({ extensions: [".webp", ".jpg"] })); ``` I've added two images to a markdown file: ```markdown ``` Note that the order of the formats in the `imagick` attribute differ. I also don't have any `imagick` shared data, so the imagick plugin shouldn't do anything other that what's set up by the picture plugin. This generates the following output: ```html ``` For the first image, the JPEG format is included twice: once in a `source` element, and once in an `img` element. However, the `img` element refers to the original image, not the transformed image. As this isn't generated or copied into the build folder, this `img` returns a 404 (but the first `source` element is selected and displayed). Also, the `webp`, included in the second `source` element format will never get used, as browsers that support this format also support JPEGs, so the first `source` element is always used. The order of source elements is fine for the second image, however, this still has the issue that the `src` for the `img` element doesn't exist. Also, I think it would be preferable for this `src` to point to the generate JPEG, since this would have broader browser support. The seems picture plugin adds `source` elements for each format defined in the `imagick` attribute, but it also copies the original `img` as-is. I think it would be better for the plugin to disregard the original `img` element and select one of the formats in the `imagick` attribute to be used as the source for a new `img` element that is generated along with the `source` elements. There are a couple of things that would need a little care here: 1. If generating a new `img` element, which source format should be used? It might make sense to specify an ordered list of preferred `img` source formats (something like `["jpeg", "png", "webp", "avif"]`, perhaps). The first matching source should be used. If there are multiple sizes, then I'd suggest the largest image size is selected. 2. If generating a new `img` element, we'd loose any attributes on the original `img` element. It might make sense to introduce a `copyAttributes` option, like the one provided by the inline plugin. Rather than creating a new `img` element, an alternative approach might be to update the `src` for the original `img` element to point to a generated image. Note that what I've described above is for the case of a single image with. I think it's correct to still include a `source` for all image formats, even the one used for the `img` element, if there are multiple sizes in play. However, in the example above, there's an unnecessary `source` element. I hope this example illustrates the issue I've seen. Please let me know if you need any more information. --- As an aside, I did try to use an attribute of `imagick="webp jpg"`, i.e. without a width specified, as all I'm trying to do is generate multiple image formats, and I'd like to retain the original sizes of images. I don't think this is currently supported by the picture plugin. Is that correct?

Title: Some images not generated when using imagick and picture plugins
Author: cgwrench
Body: ### Version 1.18.5 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? I'm trying to use the imagick and picture plugins and have found that these don't always process images as I'd expect. If I configure the imagick plugin with the following data: ```yaml imagick: resize: 300 format: webp ``` And include the following image in a HTML page: ```html ``` I get the image `fire.webp` generated in my `_site` directory. I would expect to get two images: `fire.webp` and `/fire-300w.avif`. If I change my imagick data to an array, for example: ```yaml imagick: - resize: 300 format: webp ``` I do get the two images that I expect. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? _No response_ ### What is the expected behavior? _No response_ ### What do you see instead? I would expect to see the superset of transforms defined in the imagick and picture plugin configuration used to generate images. ### Additional information I _think_ this is because of [the handling of page data in the picture plugin](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/picture.ts#L70C1-L74): ```typescript const imagick: Transformation[] = page.data.imagick ? Array.isArray(page.data.imagick) ? page.data.imagick : [page.data.imagick] : (page.data.imagick = []); ``` When `page.data.imagick` is an object, not an array, a new array is created containing that object, but the page data is never updated with the newly created array.

Title: Simplify Twitter Card Tags and Remove Unused Keywords Meta Tag
Author: ghost
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

## Issue Description

The current implementation of the `metas.ts` plugin in Lume includes unnecessary Twitter Card tags (`twitter:*`) even when OpenGraph tags (`og:*`) are present. Twitter provides fallback behavior for OpenGraph tags, which means that we don't need to duplicate our efforts by including both sets of tags.

Additionally, the keywords meta tag is no longer utilized by search engines for SEO purposes. Therefore, it can be safely removed to reduce output sizes and maintain consistent development practices.

## Proposed Changes

- Remove the `twitter:title` tag if `og:title` is present, optimizing the Twitter Card tags.
- Simplify Twitter Card tags to include only `twitter:site` and `twitter:card` when `og:*` tags are present, eliminating redundant tags.
- Consider removing the keywords meta tag as it is no longer effective for SEO, resulting in a cleaner codebase and reduced output size.

## Additional Information

- [Twitter Card Markup Documentation](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/overview/markup) provides details on Twitter's card markup requirements.
- [Getting Started with Twitter Cards](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-for-websites/cards/guides/getting-started) offers guidance on implementing Twitter Cards effectively.
- [Social Media Meta Tags](https://head-2ml.pages.dev/#social-media) provides insights into best practices for social media meta tags.
- [Google's Statement on Keywords Meta Tag](https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2009/09/google-does-not-use-keywords-meta-tag) clarifies that Google does not use the keywords meta tag for ranking websites.
- [Twitter Confirmation on Keywords Meta Tag](https://twitter.com/ChristiJOlson/status/1266415751172247552) highlights that Twitter also does not use the keywords meta tag.

These changes will streamline the meta tag implementation, reduce redundancy, and ensure alignment with current SEO practices and social media card standards. 😄

Title: Page with `url: false` not retured by `search.pages`
Author: hannobraun
Body:
### Version

1.18.5

### Platform

Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Search for pages by tag in a JSX template (e.g. `search.pages("update")`).
2. Confirm that this works as expected.
3. Set `url: false` for those pages.

### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

Reproduces every time.

### What is the expected behavior?

I expect the behavior of `search.pages` to not change, as [the documentation states](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/urls/#setting-url-to-false) that "\[the page\] is still visible by other pages, for example, in paginations"

### What do you see instead?

The same call to `search.pages` that previously worked as expected now returns an empty array.

### Additional information

I did some digging around the code. The `Searcher` [gets a reference to `this.pages` in the `Site` constructor](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/b4b12ce0c11ff64d52703566463575bf6e40f415/core/site.ts#L190), then `this.pages` is [later modified to remove pages with `url === false`](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/b4b12ce0c11ff64d52703566463575bf6e40f415/core/site.ts#L642-L648). This looks like it would also modify the array that the `Searcher` got. Maybe the `Searcher` needs a copy of that array, not a reference to it? (Sorry if this analysis is wrong. My JavaScript skills are very rusty, and my TypeScript skills very new.)

Title: Co-locating imported CSS files with the PostCSS plugin
Author: cgwrench
Body:
I'm getting started with Lume and trying to locate `@import`-ed CSS files in the same directory as the files that import them. I'm using the PostCSS plugin.

All the examples and documentation I've found so far place the imported files in a different directory, usually in a sub-directory of `_includes`. Is there a way to have CSS files and the files they import in the same directory?

For example, the [Lume base blog example](https://github.com/lumeland/base-blog) has a main CSS file, `styles.css`, in the root of the repository, which imports other CSS files from an `_includes/css` directory. These files are structured as follows:

```
├── _includes
│   └── css
│       ├── navbar.css
│       ├── post-list.css
│       └── …
└── styles.css
```

`styles.css` contains `@import` at-rules like:

```css
…
@import "css/reset.css";
@import "css/navbar.css";
…
```

I'd prefer to have files structured as follows:

```
└── css
    ├── navbar.css
    ├── post-list.css
    ├── …
    └── styles.css
```

And my `styles.css` would contain `@import` at-rules like:

```css
…
@import "reset.css";
@import "navbar.css";
…
```

In other words, the imports are resolved relative to the file containing the imports, not relative to the `_includes` directory.

I've managed to get this working with the following configuration:

```js
site
  .use(postcss({
    includes: "css/" // trailing '/' matters here
  }))
```

Note that the trailing `/` matters here - without it, no CSS files are created in the `_site` directory. This feels like a bit of a hack - is this the way to achieve the file structure I'm looking for, or is there a better way?

Title: Support content blocks?
Author: mlanza
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Nunjucks has a feature called blocks.  It's useful when you have different sections of a certain page type.  Without blocks you can inline your markdown in the yaml.  

```yaml
credits: |
  * <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
  * <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
```

Inlining in the yaml is fine with small bits of content but is awkward when you have several sections each with substantial content.  Blocks are better for this.

```md
{% block credits %}
* <label>Designers</label>: Michael Kiesling & Wolfgang Kramer
* <label>Illustrators</label>: Paul Mafayon & Christophe Swal
{% endblock %}
```

The blocks feature exists in Nunjucks but it didn't seem to work for me, so I wasn't sure if Lume as a wrapper implements it.  I didn't see any examples taking advantage of blocks, even in the other templating languages.  

I saw it rejected in #314 but I wasn't sure if it was the feature as a whole or a user-specific issue, since it seems relatively important when it comes to structured content writing.  Maybe one of the other templating languages supports the concept?  With the current design, I have pages with 3-4 sections in which I have to jam much of the prose into the front matter.  I was looking at it and it didn't seem right my content should live up in the yaml.

Title: Import NPM modules using ranges
Author: binyamin
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

### Summary
When importing an NPM module, use a caret range (ie. `npm:preact@^10.16.0`). If a user has another compatible version of the module installed, Deno will just use that one instead of downloading another one. In other words, Deno's npm specifiers support semver ranges, so let's use them.

### Notes

Esbuild imports should probably use a tilde, so only patch updates will be affected, because [esbuild makes breaking changes in minor updates](https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0190).

#### Syntax
- caret "^" allows minor & patch updates
- tilde "~" only allows patch updates

Title: Share optimized image into several locations if size and format are identical

The problem is that files `my-pic-300w@2.png` and `my-pic-600w.png` are completely identical but they generated twice, stored in cache twice, stores in fs twice and loaded into client twice. Maybe it have sence to resolve file size and share one file into several locations? So in my example instead of having 4 files we will get only 3: `my-pic-300w.png`, `my-pic-600w.png`, `my-pic-1200w.png`.

Title: Slugification for copied files
Author: svlauer
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Thank you for creating lume, I love it so far!

One thing that would be great would be a way to slugify also the names of `copy()`ed files. Because right now, the interaction of copying and slugification seems kind of bad.

Unless I am missing something (which might well be!), right now, the behavior is the following (assuming the slugify plugin is loaded):

* Suppose in my `.src` directory contains a path `with spaces and Capitals/`, which contains:
    *  a static file `with spaces and Capitals/more spaces and Capitals.bin` (with `site.copy(['.bin']`)
    *  a processed file  `with spaces and Capitals/With Spaces.md`
* I'll be getting two directories in `_site`:
    *  `with spaces and Capitals`, which contains `more spaces and Capitals.bin`
    *  `with-spaces-and-capitals`, which contains `with-spaces.html`
* Ideally, I would like to have:
    * One directory,  `with-spaces-and-capitals`, which contains `more-spaces-and-capitals.bin` and  `with-spaces.html`
* I know I can fiddle with the url of the `copy()`ed files via the second argument to `copy()`, but such a solution would be cumbersome and somewhat brittle (e.g., suppose the slugify options change).

I guess one of the reasons for the current behavior is that `copy()`ed files are not processed at all, hence the slugify plugin has no chance to intervene.

Maybe it would be an option to provide (in the slugify plugin?) a `copyAndSlugify()` method?

Or is my "ideally" above already possible, I just missed how?

If not, I'd be willing to work on a pull request for this.

Title: RFC: Putting code, tests and docs of plugins next to each other
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
The files making up a plugin are currently scattered across three different locations:

* `plugins/` in the lume repository contains the actual code, e.g. `plugins/katex.ts`
* `tests/` in the lume repository contains the test code, e.g. `tests/katex.test.ts`
* [`plugins/` in the lume.land repository](https://github.com/lumeland/lume.land/tree/6b1fddaf01c19d3c9d58c20d38d8a4dd526054b3/plugins) contains the documentation, e.g. `plugins/katex.md`

I think it would make sense to instead put all these files next to each other in this repository, e.g. we could have:

* `plugins/katex/katex.ts`
* `plugins/katex/katex.test.ts`
* `plugins/katex/README.md`

The reasoning being that if you update the code, you likely also should update the tests and the documentation as well and that's easier to do when the files are all next to each other. Putting them next to each other also makes it easier to spot discrepancies. (Especially having the .md files in another repository makes contributing to plugins more difficult since you'd often need two PRs.)

Author: not-my-profile
Body:
YAML front matters such as the following:

    ---

    ---

previously resulted in:

which as it turns out was caused by this Deno standard library peculiarity:

    undefined
    > parse('# test')
    null

Title: Dots in filenames lead to missing `.html` extension
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
Like any other static-site generator Lume by default processes `foo.md` to `foo/index.html`. However if the filename contains a dot, e.g. `foo.bar.md` then Lume currently writes the HTML to `foo.bar` ... rather than writing to `foo.bar/index.html` as you would expect (case in point Jekyll and Eleventy and I'd assume most other static-site generators behave such by default).

It's worth noting that setting `prettyUrls: false` doesn't change the Lume behavior: it still writes the HTML to `foo.bar` rather than `foo.bar.html`, as you would expect. The relevant code appears to be the `getUrl` function in `core/source.ts`.

While changing the Lume default behavior regarding this would be a breaking change, I'd consider conforming to user expectations and common practice to be enough justification.

As a workaround you can currently [specify a relative URL](https://lume.land/docs/creating-pages/urls/#relative-urls) in the page frontmatter, such as `url: ./foo.bar/`.

Title: Make `Site.copy` support multiple destinations
Author: not-my-profile
Body: Fixes #428.

Title: Cannot copy one file to multiple destinations
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
The `Site.copy` API is currently quite the footgun:

* You cannot copy one file to multiple locations.

   ```
   site.copy("foo", "bar1")
   site.copy("foo", "bar2")
   ```

   `foo` is only copied to `bar2`.

* You cannot copy a directory and one file in the directory elsewhere.

   ```
   site.copy("static")
   site.copy("static/foo", "bar")
   ```

   `static/foo` is not copied to `bar`.

I would consider both of these to be bugs.

Title: Warnings and a strict mode
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
With Lume there are currently many opportunities to make small mistakes that will not be reported. For example:

* Mistyping the source path given to `Site.copy` (if the file doesn't exist, nothing will happen).
* Using `Site.copy` multiple times for the same source path with different destinations (only the last call will take effect).
* Making a typo in a variable in a nunjuck template (i.e. referencing undefined data).
* Making a typo when specifying a tag name (either in the metadata or when calling e.g. `search.pages`).

I think it would be nice if such likely usage errors would be detected and reported. The CLI could even have a `--strict` mode that could fail if any such problem was to be detected for usage within CIs. Detecting the mistyped tags would require the ability to optionally declare them with e.g. `lume({tags: ["post"]}, ...)`.

What do you think about this matter?

Title: fix: Site.copy doing nothing for trailing slashes
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
Previously

    site.copy("static/");

had no effect whatsoever because of the trailing slash.

This commit fixes this bug to recognize the trailing slash and remove it. The trailing slash is still significant since:

    site.copy("foo/");

will now only copy a directory "foo" but not a file named "foo" (as you would expect).

Fixes #425.

Title: Trailing slash breaks `Site.copy`
Author: not-my-profile
Body:
I tried to follow the [*Copy static files* documentation](https://lume.land/docs/configuration/copy-static-files/) but wrote:

```ts
site.copy("static/");
```

instead of:

```ts
site.copy("static");
```

which apparently causes the method call to have no effect whatsoever.

Title: File deleted from _site/ during watch if not changed
Author: fserb
Body:
### Version

1.17.0

### Platform

mac

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

This is my minimal repro. `_config.js`:

```js
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";

const site = lume();
site.copy([".js"]); // it also works if you do terser, for example, and also with css copy or css postcss.
export default site;
```

create a file `a.js` with a anything on it and run `lume -w`.

If you check, there will be a `_site/a.js` file with the correct content.

Now in a separate terminal, run `touch a.js`. Lume will show: 
```
Changes detected:
- /a.js
```

but now the file disappears from `_site`. If you actually change `a.js` content, then everything works as expected (the file gets updated).



### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition?

I tried to look into the code and I have no idea how this can be happening, so maybe is some weird OS-specific, thing? I've created a new project from scratch and I'm always able to repro.

### What is the expected behavior?

The file to stay on `_site` when it changed but the content hasn't changed.

### What do you see instead?

The file disappears from `_site` until I re-run lume.

### Additional information

_No response_

Title: Search plugin: support logical NOT
Author: kwaa
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details: ### Discussed in https://github.com/lumeland/lume/discussions/405
Originally posted by **kwaa** March 31, 2023 I use [my own Frontmatter format](https://fff.js.org), where [`flags: ['unlisted']`](https://fff.js.org/concepts/flags.html) means that this post is not included in the list. So how do I accurately search for flags that contain unlisted? [The search plugin's documentation](https://lume.land/plugins/search/) doesn't seem to indicate this, or maybe it doesn't support it yet.
In this discussion I proposed `flags!*=unlisted`, but the logical NOT is more general. Example: ``` category^=A => !category^=A category$=B => !category$=B title*=security => !title*=security level>2 => !level>2 ```

Title: How to use the non-minified dev environment in client side react, to get full error messages ?
Author: max-l
Body:
I followed this example to have client side react code in a project:  https://github.com/lumeland/react-todo

And I am getting "minified" react errors, that suggest using a non minified build, to get full error and warning messages.

```
Uncaught Error: Minified React error #423; visit https://reactjs.org/docs/error-decoder.html?invariant=423 for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.
```

Title: Allow filename dates to be followed by either an underscore or hyphen
Author: paulrobertlloyd
Body:
## Description

Many (most?) static site generators use a filename format where any date time is separated from the post slug using a hyphen.

[Jekyll](https://jekyllrb.com/docs/posts/#creating-posts):

> To create a post, add a file to your `_posts` directory with the following format:
>
> ```
> YEAR-MONTH-DAY-title.MARKUP
> ```

[Hexo](https://hexo.io/docs/writing#Filename):

> By default, Hexo uses the post title as its filename. You can edit the `new_post_name` setting in `_config.yml` to change the default filename. For example, `:year-:month-:day-:title.md` will prefix filenames with the post creation date. 

[Eleventy](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/dates/):

> If a date key is omitted from the file, we then look for a `YYYY-MM-DD` format anywhere in the file path (even folders).

(I can’t find a specific documented example of the full format but in my own testing, I note that using an underscore between the date and slug doesn’t work, and Eleventy will assume that the date is part of the slug.)

This means that, if you want to port an existing site over to Lume, you will need to update post filenames, which is one more thing to think about.

By parsing filenames using an explicit regex that matches the documented formats (dates `yyyy-mm-dd` or datetimes: `yyyy-mm-dd-hh-ii-ss`), dates can be followed by either an underscore (`_`) or a hyphen (`-`).

Title: The date plugin should support specifying a timezone
Author: andreubotella
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Currently, the date plugin uses the system's default timezone. This can result in a difference between local testing and deployment, if the build for deployment uses CI (Github Actions uses the UTC timezone, for example, [although this is configurable](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/set-timezone)).

The timezone to use should be able to be set in the plugin's options, but it would also be nice to have a format that will display a date as with `Date.prototype.toISOString`. This is equivalent to `date("ATOM")` in a UTC timezone, but since `toISOString` is a standard, it would be useful to also make it available regardless of the timezone set in the plugin options.

Title: Passing `Data` to components breaks their behavior
Author: naiyerasif
Body:
### Version 1.15.1 ### Platform Windows ### What steps will reproduce the bug? Currently, Lume injects the components in the templates so that they are easily accessible by the layouts and templates. For example, I have an `_components/icon.ts` as follows. ```ts import { attributes } from "lume/plugins/attributes.ts" const defaults = { class: ["icon"], role: "img" } export default function (name: string, { title, ...attribs }: { title?: string; [key: string]: unknown }) { const href = `#${name}` const props = attributes({ ...attribs, ...defaults }) if (title) { return ` ` } else { return ` ` } } ``` Lume fails to render the component when I try to use it in a template through `comp` as described by the [docs](https://lume.land/docs/core/components/#create-your-own-components), for example, like this. ```ts export const title = "Home" export const renderOrder = 1 export default function ({ comp }) { const icon = comp.icon("moon", { slot: "dark" }) console.log(icon) return `

${title}

${icon}` } ``` The issue originates because of the ` ...attribs` specified in the component as I sometimes expect dynamic HTML attributes to be passed to the component (for example, `slot` in this case). Unfortunately, Lume also passes the entire `Data` object to the component and the `attributes` plugin fails to handle it. This also leads to unexpected issues. For example, sometimes, I may pass a `title` attribute to the component. But when I export a `title` in the template, that's also being implicitly passed to the component which is definitely not correct. ### How often does it reproduce? Is there a required condition? Reproducing this does not require anything specific. Here's the repository with the reproduction: https://github.com/naiyerasif/lume-components-issue Launch the application through `deno task serve` and you should encounter the issue described above. ### What is the expected behavior? The components should receive only those arguments which are explicitly defined in their contract. The `Data` object should not be passed to them at all. ### What do you see instead? I get the following error on console. ```sh Error: Error rendering this page - page: /index.tmpl.ts at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/renderer.ts:138:19) at async concurrent (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/utils.ts:193:3) at async Renderer.renderPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/renderer.ts:126:7) at async Site.#buildPages (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/site.ts:590:5) at async Site.build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/site.ts:518:9) at async build (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/cli/build.ts:36:3) at async Command.execute (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1794:7) at async Command.parseCommand (https://deno.land/x/cliffy@v0.25.7/command/command.ts:1639:14) at async (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/cli.ts:158:3) Caused by TypeError: value.replace is not a function at escape (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:124:16) at joinAttributes (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:117:29) at attributes (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/attributes.ts:27:10) at default (file:///E:/web/lume-components-issue/src/_components/icon.ts:7:16) | 6 | const href = `#${name}` | 7 | const props = attributes({ ...attribs, ...defaults }) | ~ | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^ | 8 | at ModuleEngine.renderSync (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/plugins/modules.ts:38:9) at (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/component_loader.ts:100:20) at Object.render (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/component_loader.ts:98:32) at Proxy. (https://deno.land/x/lume@v1.15.1/core/source.ts:542:60) at default (file:///E:/web/lume-components-issue/src/index.tmpl.ts:5:20) | 4 | export default function ({ comp }) { | 5 | const icon = comp.icon("moon", { slot: "dark" }) | ~ | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^ | 6 | console.log(icon) ``` ### Additional information _No response_

Title: Improve choosing plugins/dependencies in init script
Author: tristanisham
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Hello all,

The most important interaction a new user has with a tool like Lume is its init script. A comprehendible, quick, and instructive experience can mean the difference between a lifelong user and a permanent dismissal. My first time experience using Lume was not the former. 

I’d like to update the initialization process to make creating a new Lume site simple. To do this, it’s like to add:

- A dynamic TUI where users can choose as many plugins as they want by arrowing through a list and toggling any with “space”
- A check to determine if any plug-in dependencies are missing and a prompt to automatically add and order them. 
- Instructions, informing the user to run `deno task serve` to initialize their project. 
- General error message cleanup and the addition of suggestions on how to fix errors when they occur. 

I hope you find these suggestions well, and hopefully can expect a pull request in the coming weeks.

Title: Access rendered content from outer pages
Author: valpackett
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Inside the page layout, `{{ content }}` is the rendered content, already processed with all the `templateEngine`s.

Within `search.pages` results, there is only entirely unprocessed content, and one has to basically redo the entire pipeline manually. And I can't even get the `njk` filter—maybe due to `{% import %}` stuff in the content—to work in that context.

It would be great if the rendered content was cached and available for access in pages with higher `renderOrder`.

Title: Resolve file URLs in source-map sources
Author: binyamin
Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

Sometimes, a source-map uses stringified file URLs in the "sources" field. Sass does this. On Linux, we get an error when we try to fetch the sources' contents, in order to write the actual source-map file. Somehow, the full posix path is appended to the root file URL. So I made sure we convert file URLs to paths.

For example, say we have a file "src/style.scss" in the working directory "/home/binyamin/project". The source is resolved to "**file:///home/binyamin/project**/home/binyamin/project/src/style.scss".

Title: Allow to define data with `site.data()` to a folder/file
Author: oscarotero
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

`site.data()` is a function to assign a shared data to the site. For example:

```js
site.data("site_name", "My Blog");
```

This assign the variable `site_name` to all pages with the value "My Blog". It is similar to include this variable in a `_data.yml` file in the project root.

The idea is to be able to assign data only to a directory or even a page. For example:

```js
site.data("site_name", "My Blog"); // Assign data to the whole site
site.data("site_name", "My Gallery", "/photos"); // Assign data to the "/photos" folder
site.data("site_name", "My Photo", "/photos/photo1.md"); // Assign data to the "/photos/photo1.md" file
```

Author: pluiedev

## Description This PR makes filters registered by plugins and by the user available as a global variable in Pug templates. Previously, filters are already exposed as Pug filters, which come with the unfortunate downside that all inputs must be in plain text and no dynamic inputs are allowed. With this approach, dynamic variables can be used in filters like other template engines. Example: ```pug h1= filters.date(new Date(), "HUMAN_DATETIME") ``` Output: ```html

Title: Headless WordPress plugin
Author: oscarotero

Title: Use property alias for `metas` plugin
Author: oscarotero
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

The `metas` plugin has the `defaultPageData` option to configure default values for the `metas` properties. I think this is not convenient due two reasons:

- It cannot be configured per page because it's defined in the `_config.ts` file
- If some defaults values are defined in a `_data` file (for example, `metas.title`), then you need to do `metas.title = null` to use the default value configured.

I propose to change the plugin to implement a syntax to define aliases to existing variables. For example:

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: $title
  description: $description
```

Any value starting with `$` sign would be interpreted as an alias to an existing value (in the example above, `title` and `description`. It could allow also values like `$header.title`.

This allows to have different defaults for different types of pages, using `_data` files in subfolders.

**Edit:**

Maybe the sign `~` is better, because it's what's used by the `url` plugin to refer to source files (like `~/about-me.md`):

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: ~title
  description: ~description
```

Other alternative syntaxes:

```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: &title
  description: &description
```
(Cons: The `&` sign is a thing in Yaml).


```yml
title: Hello world
description: This is my site

metas:
  title: {{ title }}
  description: {{ description }}
```
(Const: It looks like nunjucks, people may think it's possible to use filters and other nunjucks features).

Any feedback is welcome!

Title: Allowing filters to use dynamic variables in Pug
Author: pluiedev
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Pug has [a filter system](https://pugjs.org/language/filters.html) that is currently hooked up to Lume's filters, that other template engines like Nunjucks also use. However, Pug limits filter inputs to plain text only, which is incredibly inconvenient for Lume users.

For example, the `date` filter added by [the Date plugin](https://lume.land/plugins/date/) can be used in Pug like this:
```pug
:date(pattern="HUMAN_DATETIME") 2022-11-22 7:00:30Z
```
However, this does not work with Pug variables (so something like `:date()= variable` does not work), and that is by design: 
> **Warning**
> **Filters are rendered at compile time.** This makes them fast, but it also means that they cannot support dynamic content or options.
>
> By default, compilation in the browser does not have access to JSTransformer-based filters, unless the JSTransformer modules are explicitly packed and made available through a CommonJS platform (such as Browserify or Webpack). In fact, the page you are reading right now uses Browserify to make the filters available in the browser.
>
> Templates pre-compiled on the server do not have this limitation.

As Lume precompiles templates as an SSG, I think we should lift this limitation and bring Pug in line with other template engines like Nunjucks.

Title: Replace Page.isHtml with Page.contentType
Author: Protonull
Body:
### Enter your suggestions in details:

Just saw the [changelog](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/v1.13.0/CHANGELOG.md#1130---2022-11-16) which included the addition of `Page.isHtml`, which is great, but I wonder if a broader is-function might be more useful. Lume's docs mention the possibility of dynamically generated assets [here](https://lume.land/docs/core/processors/#create-or-remove-pages-dynamically), and those assets could be blobs like PDFs of other files, or text files, indeed like the example'd Javascript-map file. But what if it's an XML file like an RSS feed? Would that appear to Lume as a HTML file? Would that therefore impact how that file is rendered? I'm not sure tbh, but I do think either way there's enough of a reason to allow for more exhaustive check. You could even keep the `Page.isHtml` by having its value set via `Page.isHtml = Page.contentType === "text/html";` You could check the prefix of the content-type to know how to save the file: if the type is prefixed with `text/` then you know that you can safely use `Deno.writeTextFile()` instead of another method.

Title: nunjucks template-inheritance/block not working as intended
Author: angelside
Body:
### Version

1.12.1

### Platform

Arch Linux

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

```shell
~ git clone https://github.com/angelside/lume-block-test.git
~ deno task serve
```

### What do you see instead?

```
Caused by Template render error: (/home/angelside/code/static/lume-block-test/index.njk)
  Error: no super block available for "breadcrumb"
```

### Additional information

Block and super structure not working with default frontmatter layout definition. 

But, it's working if replacing frontmatter with classic nunjucks extends _(Which is not the desired method, I like to use single data.yml file for layout)_.

```
---
layout: layout.njk
---
```

```
{% extends "_includes/layout.njk" %}
```

Docs: https://mozilla.github.io/nunjucks/templating.html#template-inheritance
Minimal example: https://github.com/angelside/lume-block-test

-------

layout.njk

```njk
<div class="breadcrumb">
    {% block breadcrumb %}
        block in base
    {% endblock %}
<div>
```

index.njk

```njk
{% block breadcrumb %}
    {{ super() }}
    block in index
{% endblock %}
```

Title: update jsx plugin setup to fix linting errors
Author: HoverBaum
Body:
<!--Please read the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)-->

## Description

<!-- Write a brief description of the changes introduced by this PR -->

This PR updates the created `deno.json` and `import_map.json` files when using the JSX plugin to support TypeScript types for `SX.IntrinsicElements`.

## Related Issues

Before this any TSX template would throw this error:

```
JSX element implicitly has type 'any' because no interface 'JSX.IntrinsicElements' exists.
```

This update follows a discussion on Discord: https://discord.com/channels/794537085641818124/1040217450358255617

Title: Modification of the translation system
Author: AugustinMauroy
Body:
# how it is now

https://lume.land/plugins/multilanguage/

# how it could be

> This change would make the work easier for the translators.

What I propose is simply that we have a `json` that contains the list of languages ex:`En, Fr, Es, De` and the date format. This file would be the config of the translation. Then to translate a content file you have to create a file adding the language ex:`about.en.md` => `about.fr.md`.

# Crowdin

with this relatively simple system we could add a crowdin integration.

Author: DrSensor
Body:
I just notice `deno run` support evaluating from stdin. So I think it's better to use it because you can write the lock file and reload the dependencies. For example:
```sh
echo "import 'lume/task.ts'" | deno run --reload --lock=lock.json --lock-write -
```
> **Note** there is a `-` in the end of line

This is better since `deno eval` doesn't support `--lock`/`--lock-write` and `--reload` flag. Some people (including me) might want to keep the lock file to comply with [reproducible build](https://reproducible-builds.org/) philosophy without vendoring the whole dependencies.

Here is my deno.json
```json
{
  "importMap": "build.importmap",
  "tasks": {
    "lume": "echo \"import 'lume/task.ts'\" | deno run --unstable --allow-read --allow-run --lock=lock.json -",
    "update": "deno cache --unstable --lock=lock.json --lock-write --reload _config.ts && echo \"import 'lume/task.ts'\" | deno run --unstable --allow-read --allow-run --lock=lock.json --lock-write --reload - --version"
  }
}
```

Title: Remove `page.dest`.
Author: oscarotero
Body:
Currently, the `Page` class has the `dest` property with info about the destination.

The `dest` value must be in sync with the `url` value. For example, a page with the url `/foo/` will have the following dest:

```js
{
  path: "/foo/index",
  ext: ".html"
}
```

This is why we have the `page.updateDest()` function, that change not only the `dest` value of the page but also regenerate the url  accordingly.

The problem is this only work by changing the `dest` property, but not the `url`. If the `url` of a page is changed directly, the `dest` keeps the old value.

I think `dest` is a redundant value. It should be calculated only at saving time, according to the page's `url`. So we have a single source of truth that processors and preprocessors can change at any time.

Title: Include file slug value in `Page.src` object
Author: paulrobertlloyd
Body:
Lume does some calculations behind the scenes to determine information about the source of a generated page. For example, given a file saved at `/articles/2022-08-29_porto.md`, Lume provides the following data in `Page.src`:

```js
{
  path: "/articles/2022-08-29_porto",
  lastModified: 2022-10-01T17:46:49.762Z,
  created: 2022-10-01T17:16:39.480Z,
  remote: undefined,
  ext: ".md"
}
```

Lume will also add a `page.data.data` value if the base file name contains `YYYY-MM-DD_` (so for the above this would be calculated to be `2022-10-01T00:00:00.000Z`).

However, there is one other piece of information that may be useful to site authors: the file slug. In the case of the above, this value would be `porto`.

This value could be used in `url()` functions:

```js
export function url(page) {
  return `./example/${page.src.fileSlug}/`;
}
```

It could also be used by page (pre)processors. This is my use case, where I am currently having to calculate this value myself to generate a new URL.

(Eleventy provides a similar [`fileSlug`](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/data-eleventy-supplied/#fileslug) value).

Title: Connect source-maps with source-files
Author: binyamin
Body:
### Summary
When **plugin a** creates a source-map `Page` for a given file, **plugin b** should have access to the same source-map `Page` via the given file.

### Scenario
1. User writes a file `input.scss`
2. The `sass` plugin transforms it to CSS, and adds a source-map
3. The `lightningcss` plugin minifies the CSS, and updates the source-map
4. Source-map is accurate!

### Solution
- Store the source-map on `Page` as either JSON or a string.
    - This would mean checking for a source-map when writing any file, and dealing with it accordingly.
    - This comes with the complexity of whether to write it inline or externally, and whether to add a "source-mapping URL" comment to the source's content.
- The source file's `Page` instance has a property which references a source-map `Page` instance.
    - It only needs to be one-way.
    - It should probably be a getter/setter, so we can validate it, and so it won't get in the way when debugging.

Title: Allow other ISO 8601 date representations in `date` field
Author: paulrobertlloyd
Body:
Pages can only include dates in the following formats:

* `2021-01-01`
* `2021-01-01 03:10:10`
* `2021-01-01 03:10:10 -0700`

This appears to follow RFC 3339. However, IS0 8601 dates typically don’t include spaces, but instead use `T` to separate the date and time. For example, the above can also be represented as:

* `2021-01-01`
* `2021-01-01T03:10:10Z`
* `2021-01-01Y03:10:10-0700`

Support for this representation in Lume would be useful as it would make migrating pages from an Eleventy-powered blog easier. For reference, Eleventy supports thew following [date formats](https://www.11ty.dev/docs/dates/):

* any valid [YAML date value](https://yaml.org/type/timestamp.html)
* any valid ISO 8601 string that [Luxon’s DateTime.fromISO](https://moment.github.io/luxon/#/parsing?id=iso-8601) can parse

It should be noted that Luke’s date format filters support both of the above representations (`ATOM` and `DATETIME`).

ISO 8601 dates also don’t need to include `-` or `:` separators, so the following dates are also valid:

* `20210101`
* `20210101T031010Z`
* `20210101Y031010-0700`

There may be less need to support this, although I’be come across it on my own site where I’m parsing iCalendar data which formats dates using this representation. As I can’t use them directly when generating pages, I need to manipulate them to the format Lume allows.

Title: Imagick plugin: Allow `format` property to accept an array
Author: paulrobertlloyd
Body:
Currently, `format` only allows a single value. If you want to output multiple formats, you need to declare separate transformations

```yml
imagick:
  - resize: [200, 200]
    suffix: -small
    format: jpg
  - resize: [200, 200]
    suffix: -small
    format: webp
```

To simplify the configuration of different image transformations, it would be helpful if the `format` property could accept an array. This would allow the above to be rewritten as follows:

```yml
imagick:
  - resize: [200, 200]
    suffix: -small
    format:
      - jpg
      - webp
```

Title: filterPages plugin
Author: oscarotero
Body:
A simple plugin to filter pages. It allows to ignore some pages, for example ignore `draft` pages in "prod" environment:

```ts
site.use(filterPages(
  (page) => !page.data.draft || Deno.env.get("ENV") !== "prod"
));
```

This is useful once the `dev` mode is deprecated and removed (#244).

Other option is include this functionality built-in in the Site instance, without need to import and use a plugin, example:

```ts
site.filterPages(
  (page) => !page.data.draft || Deno.env.get("ENV") !== "prod"
);
```
But not sure if this can be confused with the `site.ignore` function, very similar but it works at path level (before the page is loaded, so it can't filter by page data).

Title: Inline plugin does not respect additional style tag attributes
Author: jrson83
Body:
When using the [inline plugin](https://lume.land/plugins/inline/) with `style` tag to inline CSS, the plugin removes the actuall `style` tag from DOM and [creates a new one](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/plugins/inline.ts#L108).  This has the consequence that additional attributes are not considered or removed. For `script` tags this is not the case, since the actuall script tag gets just modified.

The plugin should respect additional attributes like `nonce="whatever"` and keep those. An example of this is the use of the `nonce` attribute for the [CSP](https://content-security-policy.com/examples/allow-inline-style/).

Title: Allow to assign multiple template engines to a format.
Author: oscarotero
Body:
It's possible to assign multiple template engines to a page, using the `templateEngine` value. For example:

```md
---
title: Hello
templateEngine: njk,md
---

# {{ title }}
```

But it's not possible to register a format to use multiple template engines. For example:

```ts
site.engine([".njk.md"], nunjucksEngine, markdownEngine);
```

This would allows to bring a initial mdx support with:

```ts
site.engine([".mdx"], jsxEngine, mdEngine);
```

Title: Remove CLI interface
Author: oscarotero
Body:
For v2.0.0 I'm thinking of removing the possibility of installing Lume with `deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume/install.ts`.

There are some reasons for this:
- Deno install doesn't work in all cases and requires to update the `PATH`.
- It's more clear and portable to update Lume by editing the `import_map.json` file. It allows to have different projects using different versions of Lume.
- Deno tasks is a better way to run Lume.
- This removes completely the bug of having two different versions of Lume (one installed globally and other different in the import_map.json).

The new workflow could be the following:

- Init a new project with `deno run -A https://deno.land/x/lume/init.ts`. This script is like `lume init`, creating the _config.ts, import_map.json and deno.json files.
- The task `deno task lume` is the main entry for all Lume comands. For example:
  - `deno task lume upgrade` To upgrade Lume (the import_map.json file)
  - `deno task lume vendor` To vendor the project.
  -  `deno task lume` To build the project
  -  `deno task lume -s` To build the project and start a server
  - etc
- The tasks `deno task build` and `deno task serve` can be keept, as an alias of `deno task lume` and `deno task lume -s` (as they are already).

The only drawback is that all projects must be initiated. It's not allowed to run Lume without the `_config.ts` file as already. But I don't think there are many cases in which the default config is enough and having a explicit `_config` file is always better.

Title: Remove Deno arguments passed by Lume
Author: oscarotero
Body:
Lume allows to pass arguments to Deno using the `--` separator. For example to use the `--compact` flag for Node compatibilty:

```
lume -s -- --compact
```

This feature was introduced before Deno tasks and Deno config file. Now this can be done editing the deno.json config file. So this feature (that is not documented) must be removed.

Title: Reject invalid url slugs

## Testing

Title: Invalid `url` values are not caught
Author: bryophyta
Body:
When specifying a `url` for a page in its yaml header, some values will fail silently. For example:

```markdown
---
# index.md

url: 0
---

# Hello world
```

This page will be built as `_site/index.md`. I believe that this is because the top-level checks that happen on the `url` value are to see if it is `false` or has `typeof string` or `typeof function`. https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/8e6bb7b112861b2699a37d89501aeb000768298f/core/renderer.ts#L175-L220

Because values like `0` are parsed as numbers, they fail these conditions (thereby bypassing the validation that happens for strings) and end up with the default `url`.[^1] My assumption is that this is not the desired behaviour?

Do you have a sense of what the desired behaviour would be, here? I'm not sure whether it would be better for it to crash, or to warn the user, or to coerce the value to a string. But if you have a view on what the desired behaviour should be, I'd be happy to try and write up a PR to implement it.

[^1]: Similarly, if an array is provided as the `url` value, this will also fail silently and the page will be built with its default url.

Title: Warn user when empty files are skipped
Author: bryophyta
Body:
## What?

Logs a warning to tell the user when a file is being skipped from the build because it's empty.


## Why?

I was looking around the code for this bit of functionality and noticed that there was an open issue to add some logging. (Closes #180)

The issue was opened a while ago, so I don't know if it's still something you want to add. But because it was a small change I thought I might as well just submit a PR. Feel free to reject it if the issue isn't relevant anymore!


## Testing

This PR shouldn't affect functionality, and it doesn't seem like logging messages are something that should have unit tests attached to it. But if it's desirable to add a test here then I'm happy to do so.

On this topic, I was wondering if you have any tips on how to run a locally modified version of lume? I had a quick attempt but I was hitting some issues; I'm fairly new to Deno so I imagine that I'm missing something about how dependencies are resolved..

Body:
I'm using the [remark plugin](https://github.com/lumeland/experimental-plugins/tree/main/remark), which now adds `vFile.data` to the markdown `pages.data`. It is working great, so I wrote another rehype plugin, to exctract an `excerpt` (the first paragraph, with max length of 140 characters), from every blog post. The `vfile.data.excerpt` gets appended to the `page.data.excerpt`, like using the `rehype-extract-toc` plugin. Now using the `.md` files layout file, I have access to the `page.data` which includes: ```js url: "/posts/blog-post-02/", excerpt: "Lorem markdownum exclamant. Saevit iussa, in parentem praenovimus gerit sceleratior ipse ima quam\nal...", toc: [ { depth: 2, value: "Praevisos ductae et poenae esse pereunt enituntur" }, { depth: 2, value: "Illis Hyadasque dederat" }, { depth: 2, value: "Adversi firmat nigra et traiectus quos" }, { depth: 2, value: "Dignior lucis multa comitavit tenet" } ], ``` In my `index.tsx` page file, for which the `excerpt` data was supposed to be, I loop through the posts: ```js export default ({ search }: SiteData) => { const posts = search.pages("type=post", "date=desc", 3); return (

Recent blog posts

{posts.map(({ data }) => (

{data.title as string}

{data.excerpt}
))}
); }; ``` Here I don't have access to the data, since like I read in the docs [render order](https://lume.land/docs/core/render-order/), the pages didn't exist before the `index` page was rendered. I tried changing the `renderOrder` from all posts inside `./posts/_data.ts`: `export const renderOrder = -1;` But after build I can see no navbar items at all, (they are defined through `menu` inside the pages), the blog posts getting displayed, but the `page.data` still not contains `excerpt` & `toc`. When I use `export const renderOrder = 1;` inside the index page, the navbar does not contain the index page, the blog posts getting displayed, but the `page.data` still not contains `excerpt` & `toc`. Here is my `_config.ts` order: ```js site .copy("assets", ".") .use(readingTime()) .use(date()) .use(slugifyUrls()) .use(remark({ remarkPlugins, rehypePlugins, })) .use(preactjsx()) .use(postcss()) ``` If you can give any advice, I would be pleased.

Title: SASS plugin includes
Author: jrson83
Body:
# Version Info
Windows 11 (64 bit)
deno 1.23.2 (release, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc)
v8 10.4.132.8
typescript 4.7.2
lume v1.10.0

# Description

The SASS docs [Configuration](https://lume.land/plugins/sass/#configuration) chapter states:

> includes: An array of directories to search for the `@imported` files. By default is _includes.

When I move `.scss` files outside the `_includes` directory, they get transformed to `_site/whatever_dir/[file].css`, even if I do not specify any `sass.includes` path.

I created the following project structure:

```
lume-root
 ┣ _includes
 ┃ ┗ sass
 ┃ ┃ ┣ index.scss
 ┃ ┃ ┗ _test.scss
 ┣ deno.json
 ┣ import_map.json
 ┗ _config.ts
```

```scss
// index.scss
@import "./test";

body {
  background-color: #000;
}
```

```js
// _config.ts
import lume from "lume/mod.ts";
import sass from "lume/plugins/sass.ts";

const site = lume();

site.use(sass());

export default site;
```

When I run `deno task serve`, the `.scss` files are not getting transformed to `.css`.

In serve mode, if I make changes to the files they getting registered:
```shell
Changes detected:
- /_includes/sass/index.scss
```

I tried setting `sass.includes` with `./` and without `./` combinations as `string` and also as `Array<string>`, but nothing worked.

- ["./_includes/sass/"]
- ["./_includes/sass"]
- ["./_includes/"]
- ["./_includes"]

I created a different directory with an underscore `_styles` and moved the files in the directory, they not getting transformed. I also tried to specify the `sass.includes`.

So it seems, the plugins default is to search in every subdir of the rootdir, but not if the dir contains an underscore.
If you need I'll create an example for reproduction. Thanks for the great work!

Title: Support `deno.jsonc` for Deno config file
Author: binyamin
Body:
### Summary
Currently, Lume only supports `deno.json` files. Deno itself supports `deno.jsonc` files as well. Ideally, Lume would match Deno's behavior.

### Brainstorming
- Deno's standard library includes a JSONC parser in [`std/encoding`](https://deno.land/std@0.147.0/encoding/jsonc.ts). It does _not_ include a method for _serializing_ JSONC (see https://github.com/denoland/deno_std/discussions/1803).
- We only write to the deno config after the _vendor_ and _init_ commands. That's not too often. It might be worth parsing the JSONC using `std/encoding`, and doing some RegExp magic to overwrite a portion of the file. Just brainstorming here.

### Notes
- This feature is not in any way critical. I simply wanted to track JSONC support, and to explain why Lume does not in fact support JSONC.

Title: Can't copy files with prefix `_`
Author: fabon-f
Body:
# Problem

I want to copy `_headers` and `_redirects` to dest directory and tried like this in `_config.js`.

```js
site.copy("_headers")
site.copy("_redirects")
```

But these files are just silently ignored.

To my understanding, files with prefix `_` are ignored by default. But in my opinion it is better that this default behavior can be overridden by explicit config.

# Workaround for now

Renaming files and use second argument of `site.copy` works properly.

```
site.copy("headers", "_headers")
```

Title: Support global templateEngine settings with images in content folders
Author: Brixy
Body:
I am just porting another site to Lume from Eleventy. And it works just great!

The old sites uses Eleventy’s Nunjucks shortcodes within markdown files. Lume supports this with `templateEngine: njk,md` in front matter.

Yet, I noticed an issue: Apparently you cannot use this setting globally for all blog posts in combination with images in folders, probably because images are pages in Lume:

```
# posts/_data.js
export const templateEngine = 'njk,md';
```

```
posts/
└── 2022-05-28_a-post/
    ├── index.md
    ├── file.pdf
    └── image.jpg
```

As soon as you remove the image file from the folder everything works as expected.

(Honestly speaking I have not tried Lume’s modules plugin with this combination.)

Thank you.

Title: Convert string dates to `Date` automatically
Author: oscarotero
Body:
Lume expects the `date` property to be a `Date` instance. In `yaml` this is converted automatically but in JavaScript this code fails:

```js
export const date = "2022-04-15";
```

because it needs to be:

```js
export const date = new Date("2022-04-15");
```

Lume should convert valid strings to `Date` here: https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/master/core/page_loader.ts#L86

Title: JSX does not reload if included as import
Author: RitzyMage
Body:
(this may be related to [issue #135](https://github.com/lumeland/lume/issues/135)) If JSX contains a component using the comp object (e.g. ``` export default ({comp}) =>
{comp.componentName({ prop: "value", })}
``` ), then the hot reloading works just fine. Specifically, Lume outputs ``` Changes detected: - /_components/componentName.jsx 🔥 / /index.jsx Changes sent to the browser ``` However, if included via an import (e.g. ``` import ComponentName from "./_components/componentName.jsx"; export default () =>
``` ), then the reload does not work for changes in the included component (`componentName` in this example). In fact, the server must be completely restarted to get these changes. Specifically, Lume outputs ``` Changes detected: - /_components/componentName.jsx ``` but does not output the hot reloading text.

Title: Prevent overwriting the output files
Author: aiotter
Body:
Imagine you accidentally create two tags `blog` and `Blog`, and let Lume generate `_site/tags/blog/index.html` and `_site/tags/Blog/index.html` respectively (this is exactly what I did).
Case-insensitive filesystems regard those two files as the same, allowing Lume to overwrite one with the other.

This PR warns user that there already exist a generated file.

Title: `Engine.render()` and `Engine.renderSync()` take generator as its first argument
Author: aiotter
Body:
When `Page` is a generator, `Engine.render()` and `Engine.renderSync()` take generator as its first argument, and returns generator which yields `Data`.
I don't feel this intuitive. They would better to take an iterated content and data as its argument, and returns `string`.

Also, `Engine.render()` can return generator, on the other hand, `Engine.renderSync()` cannot. I don't think the typing here is correct.

Title: Merge pageLoader and assetsLoader into resourceLoader
Author: oscarotero
Body: Related to #165

Title: Fix: Assets cannot be rendered with chained extension
Author: aiotter
Body:
## Describe the bug
You cannot get "hydration.client.tsx" rendered with a `_config.ts` below.
```ts
site.loadPages([".tsx"], moduleLoader, tsxEngine)
  .loadAssets([".client.tsx"], clientSideScriptLoader)
  .process([".client.tsx"], (page) => page.dest.ext = ".js")
```

## Background
The problem on code which directory causes the bug is that pages are always preferred over assets.
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f02e6832db31cc55f6da96fcab39213e50c17411/core/source.ts#L212-L213

1d67763c198972c202aed359f1f21e9f0f076aca fixes this, however, another problem occurrs.
In the current implementation, `Site.renderer.renderPages()` renders everything with `Site.renderer.engines.render()`. So when you have a matching extension in the engines, the file will be rendered by the corresponding engine even if the file is actually an asset, not a page.
https://github.com/lumeland/lume/blob/f02e6832db31cc55f6da96fcab39213e50c17411/core/renderer.ts#L88-L97 

To prevent this, we have to use `if` clause not to execute `engines.render()`for assets. However, we have no knowledge about the file in problem is an asset or a page on the context of `Site.renderer`.
So I implemented `Site.assets` array to store assets separately from `Site.pages`. To make it more natural, I also implemented a new class `Asset`, which is an analogue of `Page`.

Title: Allow passing sourceMap options to PostCSS
Author: fserb
Body:
PostCSS accepts `boolean | SourceMapOptions` as `options.map`.
This slightly changes the default behavior, as "map: true" will default to inline.

We can change it to `options.sourceMap === true ? {inline: false} : options.sourceMap`, but I'm not sure is worth keeping the extra weirdness. WDYT?

Title: cli.utils.runWatch timeout is too long?
Author: fserb
Body:
Right now, there's a 500ms debounce timer on `runWatch()` that makes the rebuild happen 500ms after the last event has been seen.

I understand that the intention is to try to wait a bit before doing an unnecessary rebuild if multiple files are being saved. But there are two issues:

1. 500ms is a small eternity for fast save-rebuild-reload feedback (and there's also the actual build time).
2. `FSWatcher ASyncIterator` will do the right thing if multiple files are being detected at the same time. I.e., if we do *any* `setTimeout` it will happen **after** all the FSWatchers are done. The FSWatcher async iterator will always happen before (if it actually detects a file change).

I've tested this on my machine by editing multiple files and saving them all (with "save all" on my editor).
With a `setTimeout(callback, 0)` I still get a single reload that detects all the changes before (the 2 events for each file, etc).

No matter what the debounce time is, it probably won't be fast enough for multiple manual saves, so we are only really ready for automatic saves (i.e., save all). I understand that theoretically there may be machines that take XXms to save files, but that's probably not common (or at least not very relevant for save-rebuild?).

That said, I'd suggest moving this to 0, but I'd be happy with either a much much smaller number (less than 10ms?) or with a way to set this with a config. WDYT?

Title: Provide system to force reload of files
Author: amatiasq
Body:
Following https://github.com/lumeland/lume/pull/134, we need a way to force lume to reload a file.

Example use case is if we know `a.ts` depends on `b.ts` whenever we receive a `beforeUpdate` event because `b.ts` has changed we want Lume to also re-evaluate `a.ts`.

We can add `a.ts` to `event.files` but that won't trigger a re-evaluation because of `Source` internal cache.

Title: Track files' dependencies when watching filesystem
Author: amatiasq
Body:
This is implemented by changing `loader/module.ts`, adding a step before the module to be loaded is actually evaluated. We use `Deno.emit()` to bundle the module with all it's dependencies, this way Deno cache will be bypased and we'll always load the latest version from disk.

`Deno.emit()` also returns source maps for the generated bundle. We use this information to extract the dependencies of the module, this way when we detect one of such dependencies has changed we know we need to also load the dependant module.

Title: Improve metrics to be more analytics friendly
Author: shah
Body:
@oscarotero and team, I have these suggestions for improving metrics:

1. Increase granularity
2. Allow "baggage" (in open telemetry terms) to be contributed by pages

**Increasing granularity**. Right now we have the following in `metrics.json` where the "name" has event details that need to parsed in order to be consumable for analytics:

```json
{
    "name": "Load: /support/generate-static-build.sh",
    "entryType": "measure",
    "startTime": 1009.5581999999999,
    "duration": 0.1815000000000282,
    "detail": null
  }
```

Instead, I suggest something like this:

```
{
    "name": "Load: /home", // human-friendly, not useful for analytics
    "nature": { category: "load" }, // analytics-friendly, nature can be like a URL with depth /load/X, /preprocess, /render/Z, etc. and can be merged with metrics contributed by pages being rendered
    "target": { url: "/page/url", ...anything else we know about target... }, // analytics-friendly
    "entryType": "measure", // "measure" is the only entry I see here -- is there anything else?
    "startTime": 1007.8624, // analytics-friendly, 
    "duration": 2187.5929, // suggest removing "duration" and only including analytics-friendly "endTime"
    "detail": null // suggest renaming to "baggage" using OpenTelemetry terminology; and allow this to be contributed/merged from generated pages (through filter.metrics({ nature: {  }, baggage: {... anything ...}})
}
```

**Allow metrics "contribution" by pages**. Many of my pages run database calls or other expensive calls and I'd like to add my own metrics for those expensive items.

```ts
export default async function ( // for Lume
  page: lumeAide.RenderablePage,
  filters: lumeAide.Filters,
) {
  // do something expensive
  filter.metrics({ nature: {  }, baggage: {... anything ...}}); // I can call my own metrics?
}
```

Title: Is _data.* read multiple times or is it a singleton?
Author: shah
Body:
I am using `_data.ts` to setup my common data across pages. One of my variables is _sourced_ from a PostgreSQL database so in `_data.ts` I connect to the database, read the content, and store in a variable that I then use across multiple pages. Everything is working well with respect to generating HTML.

What I noticed was that `_data.ts` seems to be sourced for each page (and is running my database connection for each page). Since the data is common to all pages, I assumed `_data.ts` would only be loaded once and then the variables are made available to all page templates but that does not seem to be the case.

So I'm wondering is `_data.*` _meant_ to sourced once for _each page_ or once _per build_? For now I've solved my problem by creating a singleton in `_data.ts` but wanted to report this issue to see what you thought.

Title: Always print errors with stack
Author: fserb
Body: This addresses bug #116

Title: Understanding most errors is hard because of printError()
Author: fserb
Body:
I think Lume is used by people who are somewhat familiar with Deno (at least you need to be familiar enough to create a config file and most documentation assumes one can code a bit of Javascript).

That said, I think hiding the exception stack by default is a bad idea.

When I have a problem in my config, most of the time there's no good info on `printDataError`, and I end up getting something like:

```
Error: Cannot read property 'toLowerCase' of undefined

Task completed in 0m1.283s
```

This is unexpected and not really useful since most of the time the error message (even if it had the accompanying src page file that caused it) will still not uniquely identify where the error is coming from. 

I think it's a fair effort to try to associate a particular error to a source page file (that `printDataError` does) and I think Lume should definitely keep doing this and I'll try to track why I'm not getting it on those cases: I'm not sure if it's data files or preprocessors, or a Markdown/Nunjuck plugin. This reinforces my point! I got an error and I have no clue where the error is even coming from. Did I mess up my config? Is the plugin I'm using broken? Did I forgot a field somewhere?

I know I'm able to set `{verbose: 2}`, but my point is a bit deeper than that. Deno errors are not made to be presented without the stack. They make almost as much sense as just writing "Error" and nothing else.

I think we should remove the `debug` option on `printError()` and always output the full exception. It's usually not that verbose (2 to 10 lines); even non "core" developers can understand what it is (and most people using Lume will be familiar with them); it makes the error messages meaningful; and it makes it easier for users to create issues when it comes to that.

The way it currently is, we are almost saying _"there's an error, I'll sometimes tell you what is the offending file, but in any case change something until it works"_.

Title: JavaScript source maps contain incorrect URL
Author: valtlai
Body:
Source maps generated by the Terser plugin have an incorrect URL `0` in the `sources` array.

A test case:

* `_config.js`:

  ```js
  import lume from "lume/mod.js";
  import terser from "lume/plugins/terser.js";
  
  const site = lume()
    .use(terser({ sourceMap: true }));
  
  export default site;
  ```

* `index.njk`:

  ```html
  <script src="/foo.js"></script>
  ```

* `foo.js`:

  ```js
  console.log("Test!");
  ```

That results the following source map:

* `foo.js.map`:

  ```json
  {"version":3,"sources":["0"],"names":["console","log"],"mappings":"AAAAA,QAAQC,IAAI","file":"/foo.js"}
  ```

  Should have `foo.js` instead of `0`.

----

**Edit**: The issue originally covered the PostCSS plugin too, but I think its source maps are correct after all.

Title: Allow setting includes directory and non-absolute copy
Author: fserb
Body:
This PR adds a `includes` option to change the `_includes` directory and allows `site.copy()` to copy from directories relative to `src` (dest is still absolute).

The rationale behind this is: for one of my projects, I have loads of top level files, I want to keep them in a subdirectory. When I change `src`, I'm kinda forced to change my assets and _include, because they "follow" the src. With this change, I'm able to specify `includes: "../_includes"` and `site.copy('../assets', 'assets')`.

Alternatively, we could make those two (includes and copy) to be relative to the execution, but I'm not sure that's a good idea, because it breaks the "I want to isolate a build" use case.

Title: lume server doesn't reset computed extraData
Author: fserb
Body:
I have a plugin that does a Table of Contents based on Headings (h1, h2, h3... instead of tags). For this, I process() each page and extract the headings. All is good and it works fine.

The problem comes when I run lume in server/watch mode.
In that case, there doesn't seem to be way to "reset" the TOC and not double it at every reload, since I need to keep it global to all pages.

If I try as a global list, it obviously doesn't get cleared.
But if I try as part of site.extraData, it also doesn't get cleared.

I think it might be overkill to have a `site.rebuild(callback)`, but it feels like this should have a proper solution? I tried using events, but `beforeBuild` is not called all the time and `beforeUpdate` only reload some files. My current solution is to find a way to update the TOC instead of adding to it.


(related, but not really, it would be really nice to have a `--watch` option that does only watch/rebuild and no HTTP server)

Title: Allow extensions on Nunjucks
Author: fserb
Body:
Extensions on Nunjucks can't be passed by regular options, they need to be added by `addExtension`, so we add a new `extensions` option and register them.

I had to move it inside the passed options, because `options.extensions` is already a thing. We could do `options.nunjucks_extensions` if you want it to be outside `options.options`. WDYT?

Title: Performance observability
Author: shah
Body:
As you start your TypeScript journey (or even before!) I suggest adding some _observability_ across all the major pipeline steps. For example, if I have 100 pages being generated but 5 of them take 95% of time to generate, we should be able to see that when the pages are being tracked. For large sites some parts of site take much longer to generate so the developer should be responsible for caching those pages but she'd need to be told which ones they are.

Something like this?

```bash
🔥 Deno prepare (900ms)
🔥 /images /images (copy: 25ms)
🔥 /index.html /index.tmpl.ts (load: 1025ms, render: 255ms, process: 190ms, etc: ??)
🔥 /styles.css /styles.css (??)

🍾 Site built into ./_site (1255ms)
```

It would be good to build [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) and [OpenMetrics](https://openmetrics.io/) (Prometheus) into Lume so that large sites can fine tune performance through observability.

Title: changed page url generation
Author: oscarotero
Body:
@valtlai After a second thought, I've changed my mind about the possibility to define an url as an object and your idea of using the trailing slash to detect if the url is a pretty-html or a extension-less filename is better.

One of my goals with lume is building something easy to use and with predictable behavior, and the url variable that accepts so many formats (function, string, object) and different behaviors is far from being that.

With this change, the url can be defined using a function or a string:

- `/about-me` Generates a `/about-me` file (without extension)
- `/about-me/` Generates a `/about-me/index.html` file
- `/about-me.html` Generates a `/about-me.html` file

`prettyUrls` setting is used only if the page has not the url variable. `slugifyUrls` is used everywhere.

Title: njk filter returns empty if there's another async filter
Author: fserb
Body:
here's the case.
If you have a config: `site.filter('x', async () => "hello", true);`
and a page A that uses `{{ x }}`.

If you need to render content from page A into page B: `{{ A.page.content }}`  (pre rendered content) will work. But `{{ A.page.content | njk }}` will return empty.

This is useful to render content of RSS feeds, where you don't want `A.content` (the full rendered page) but still want to resolve the templates.

I think the problem is that the njk plugin/filter should either assume it's async by default or have an option to become so.

Title: Optionally support async render functions for JSX pages
Author: jerluc
Body: It looks like engines already support async `render` functions, so I figured it would make sense to enable this functionality for JSX pages. Note that I'm using `await Promise.resolve(...)` as [according to the MDN docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise/resolve#parameters), this method can both safely wrap an existing Promise or a regular value. This means that this change should remain backward compatible for normal non-async page functions.

Title: Outputting extensionless file (like `_headers`) from template
Author: valtlai
Body:
I would like to dynamically generate a `_headers` file to configure HTTP headers for Netlify. (And I’m going to do something similar with `_redirects` too.)

There’s no way to output a file named `_headers` from a template (i.e. without using `site.copy()`). Setting `slugifyUrls: false` in the config file and `url: /_headers` in a front matter results a file `_headers/index.html` (or `_headers.html` if `prettyUrls: false`).

The problem is that the `_headers` file has no file extension and Lume adds `.html` for missing one.

Should we stop adding the extension and only add `index.html` if `url` has a trailing slash? Then `url: /foo` would output `/foo` and `url: /foo/` would output `/foo/index.html`. Or should there be an option (such as `forceExtensions: false`) for this behavior?

Title: Fix date creation
Author: valtlai
Body:
The `Date` constructor does not support `undefined` as an argument, so we must specify the default values ourselves.

Also
* allow a lowercase `t` as the date-time separator
* shorten the regexes by using `\d\d` instead of `\d{2}`
* reorder the comparison operators in comments to match the ones in `switch`–`case`.

Title: Date format does not behave as expected (off by one day)
Author: kevinlang
Body:
I have a template with the date specified as e.g., `2021-04-08`.

I would expect this formatting:
```
post.data.date | date('PPP')
```

To return:
```
April 8th, 2021
```

However, it returns:
```
April 7th, 2021
```

If I format with the `ATOM` format, I can see that the issue seems to be related to stripping off the timezone, or something.
```
post.data.date | date(`ATOM')
=> "2021-04-07T20:00:00-04:00"
```

(I'm in GMT-4)

Title: Add ability to change sort direction for date
Author: kevinlang
Body:
I see this documentation here: https://lumeland.github.io/creating-pages/searching/

And I see that `date` is the default sort. As far as I can tell it dates in ascending order (oldest first). I would like to sort in descending order (newest first), but could not find how to.

Perhaps looking something like:
```
search.pages("type=post", "date=desc")
```

Cheers,
